hook: The social media habit that's quietly destroying friendships.

episodes.title: The Night The Tea Session Started
episodes.hook: The text message that's quietly destroying friendships.
episodes.story: Yaar, so we all sat in the living room last night. Just five of us. Het, Priyanshi, and me. The rent is ₹25k for two rooms, and we're all freelancing, so money is tight. We started drinking chai, thinking it would be chill. But Priyanshi was looking at her phone, and Het was glaring. I asked, 'What happened?' They said 'Nothing.' But I saw the WhatsApp chat. It wasn't just a fight. It was a screenshot of a payment delay. I thought, 'Oh no, this isn't just drama.' We all earn around ₹60k, but when you split bills with two other people, trust is the real currency. I realized, this isn't about money. It's about respect. But I didn't know the worst part yet. I saw the date on the message.
episodes.cliffhanger: But the worst part? I found out it wasn't about the money at all.

episodes.title: The Real Reason They Fought
episodes.hook: The one message that changed everything.
episodes.story: Okay, so here's the tea. Priyanshi accused Het of eating her food without asking. Not once, but twice. But when I saw the chat logs, it was deeper. Priyanshi said, 'You don't care about my schedule.' Het said, 'You don't respect my work.' We're all WFH, right? So noise, time, space—everything clashes. I tried to mediate, but they were both tired. The bossy vibe was real. I remembered my own flatmate fights. It's not about the food. It's about the feeling of not being heard. I told them, 'Look, we need to sign a roommate agreement.' They laughed. But then Priyanshi told me something I didn't expect. She said Het didn't pay the last month's electricity bill. Just ₹800. But that's not the point.
episodes.cliffhanger: And part 2 reveals the exact message that started it all.

episodes.title: Who Actually Moves Out?
episodes.hook: The moment I almost shut everything down.
episodes.story: So we had to make a choice. The landlord wants rent on Friday. We can't pay if one of us leaves. Het suggested paying extra, but Priyanshi refused. I said, 'One of you has to go.' The room went silent. It's not easy. We're friends. But living together is a contract. Het said, 'I'll leave.' Priyanshi said, 'No, I'll go.' Neither wanted to. It's a 2026 reality—rent is too high to move out alone. I told them, 'Let's split the difference.' We voted. Priyanshi left. But the weird part? She didn't tell us why. She just packed up. Now, I'm sitting here alone with the two others. And I found a note on the table.
episodes.cliffhanger: The note said something that changes how I see my new flatmates.
